title: wire.phall.io core entry
description: The frozen core every entry carries, regardless of family. All category-specific data lives in `ext`, whose shape is declared by `ext_type`. Provenance fields are mandatory so an agent can trust a fact inline, without a second call.

id.description: Globally unique stable id, identical to the permalink path: '{family}[/{namespace}]/{slug}'. Primary key; never reused, never renumbered.

family.description: Closed, curated vocabulary segment. Decoupled from upstream IANA filenames.

namespace.description: Present ONLY for conditional-depth families with multiple colliding flat key-spaces (e.g. 'cipher-suites' within tls-param). Omitted for the common two-segment case.

slug.description: Family-local natural key: the number (status/port/rrtype/cbor/osc), the canonical token (method/scheme), or a kebab-cased media type (slash -> dash).

title.description: Short human/agent-readable name, e.g. 'OSC 133 — Semantic Prompts (FinalTerm shell integration)'.

summary.description: 1-3 sentence token-budgeted plain-text description, written to be quoted verbatim by an agent.

kind.description: Cross-family classifier so agents can filter without parsing family semantics.

aliases.description: Other names / abbreviations / historical labels. Powers search and disambiguation.

status.description: Standardization status of the thing itself. Distinct from `verification`.

verification.description: Trust state of THIS entry's data. An entry that is not `verified` is excluded from the default search index (trust beats breadth). `contested` flags cross-source disagreement rather than silently resolving it.

tier.description: Ingestion provenance class. A = deterministic parse of a machine-readable upstream (IANA CSV/XML); B = parsed from a structured-but-curated source (xterm ctlseqs); C = human/LLM synthesis across multiple sources.

source_url.description: PROVENANCE (mandatory). Canonical upstream URL the entry was derived from.

source_version.description: PROVENANCE (mandatory). Upstream version/edition/patch/etag/row-date. 'unversioned' allowed only when the upstream truly has none.

retrieved_date.description: PROVENANCE (mandatory). UTC date the ingester fetched / last reconciled the source.

attribution.description: PROVENANCE. MANDATORY when tier=C or the entry fuses multiple sources. Per-fact citation: each row pins one claim (by #anchor / JSON pointer) to an exact source, so an agent can trust a synthesized entry without re-researching.

claim_ref.description: The claim this row supports, e.g. '#D' or 'ext.params[3].subparams[0]'.

see_also.description: Related entry ids (not full URLs, so they stay cheap and rewrap-able), e.g. 'terminal-osc/633'.

ext_type.description: Names the versioned schema of `ext`. Lets an agent/MCP tool request and validate only the extension shape it understands.

ext.description: Typed extension block; shape selected by `ext_type`. Where all category-specific data lives so the core stays tiny and uniform. May be {} when there is no extra structured data.

updated.description: Last time THIS entry's content changed (distinct from retrieved_date). Drives ETag caching and the 'what changed' feed.
